entry.title: SCM & Livecache Export
entry.content:
Dear Team

The SCM system (QSC) is currently running on an MSSQL Server database, while the LiveCache system (QLC) is running on a MAXDB database. As per the customer's request, we are required to perform an SWPM export of the SCM system.

My question is:

When performing an SWPM export of the SCM system (which is on MSSQL Server), will the LiveCache data also be included in the export?

Or,

Do I need to perform a separate export of the LiveCache system using SWPM after completing the SCM export?

If a separate export is required, how should I export LiveCache — is it done through SWPM or using a different tool?

Regards
Appuru

entry.title: Re: Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
entry.content:
Thank you. We have received your request and put it into our planning board. The topic expert will be in touch soon.

Best regards,

Eberechukwu Nwaobiora

entry.title: Prerequisite for implement alumni for ONB2.0
entry.content: In 1H 2025 Product Release, ONB2.0 introduced alumni feature. However we would like to know how it can get implemented whether implement Offboarding is a must or it can implement alone.

entry.title: Re: Partner not reached
entry.content:
Hi Pravat_Rath ,

 

Are you using the Connect button, provide in our UI? If not, could you try again using this approach?

Are your SSH, RDP and SAP GUI ports properly restricted?

For more information how to restrict your ports, please refer to our documentation. You could try at first to restrict to your own IP and retest the connectivity. If it works for you, the you could define an appropriate IP range if it is required in your case.

More information about required credentials is available in your Appliance Template Getting Started Guide .

 

Best regards,

Tsvetinka

entry.title: Re: Generate IDOC from SAP BTP CAP app
entry.content: @ArunJacob This is outbound IDOC example, we want to send the IDOC to S4 system from BTP Cloud system

entry.title: Seeking Best Practices for Succession Management
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
 
I am looking for best practices and experiences regarding Succession Management. I have already reached out to some contacts in my professional network, but I would love to hear your opinions and suggestions as well.
I am particularly interested in the following aspects:
  • Responsibility: Who is responsible for Succession Management in your organizations? What roles and departments are involved?
  • Timeframe: What timelines do you set for planning and implementing succession processes? Are there any long-term strategies that have proven effective?
I look forward to your responses and exchanging ideas!
 
Thank you in advance!
 
Best regards from Hanover, Germany,
 
Max

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model: What’s New as of 2025-04-07?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community members! 

I am glad that I can bring to you the newest updates related to SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model for the month of March. 

Let’s dive into the latest updates! 

System > Process Scheduler  

SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model has enhanced the Process Scheduler by adding Schedule Type field to help our users to select on a specific scheduling type. The options that users can select are One Time, Repeat Interval, and Cron schedule types.    

More details about the Process Scheduler enhancements can be found in the SAP PaPM Cloud blogpost: SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model – Cron expression for Process Scheduler or visit our Application Help portal Process Scheduler 

Administration > Settings 

We are integrating SAP Cloud Transport Management (SAP cTMS) into Application Shell Integration Settings within SAP Profitability and Performance Management (SAP PaPM) to enhance user experience by providing quick and seamless access to SAP cTMS directly from SAP PaPM.  

To learn more about this setting, please refer to the Application Help through this link.  
Want to dive deeper into SAP cTMS? Feel free to check out this SAP PAPM Cloud SM integration with SAP cTMS blogpost. 

User Preferences  

Good news for Users in Poland, Polish language is now supported by SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model App.   

For more information visit User Preferences in Application Help Portal.  

Modeling > Remote Function Adapter  

To improve functionality and prevent unintended changes, we have refined the editing capabilities in the RFA mapping sheet for all RFA function types.  

Now, only the "Value" column is editable, while all others remain fixed to prevent errors and ensure stability of the Application.  

 

I hope you enjoyed this blog post and found it helpful in learning about the new features in SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model. 

To stay updated with our previous posts on the latest developments and features in SAP PaPM Cloud SM, check out the tag PaPMCloudWhatsNew, which archives all related content. 

Thank you and stay tuned for the future What's New series!

entry.title: Cost posting for professional services project
entry.content:
Hello Experts!

We are wondering if the these cost postings are correct and this it what it should look like at the end of the procces for a P&L for example. Currently it looks like this and posted for a consulting gl account:

and this is one of those journel entries:

So the question is, is this correct or is there some missing configuration or just some step missing?

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: Compensation Plan Variable Pay set different budget i...
entry.content:
Hi @AprilKam 

As far as I know we don't have a way to meet all of these requirement right now (set budget by worksheet with employees not impacting it when moving), Florin's proposed solution is the only one.

I would be curious to understand or see what your Variable Pay template looks like:

  • Do you have a business section and individual that are automatically calculated based on assignments ?
  • Or do you have only an individual section and if so how do you use it ? do you calculate payouts at all ? 

Leading practice Variable Pay budgets only apply on the individual component of the calculation. For example if for an employee the max calculated business goals payout is 3000 and the max individual goals payout is 2000 then we can set the budget for that worksheet to be the sum of all max individual component target for all employees on the worksheet (so 2000 in our example + other amounts from other employees). By combining this with guidelines we can ensure that managers can still bump team members' bonuses while sticking to budget.

There was a post about budgets in Variable Pay not long ago with a link in it to all the different types of budgets we can have in VarPay that can be of interest to you:

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-q-a/variable-pay-budget-calculation/qaq-p/14019820

All the best,
Xavier

Is there any additional setting required?

entry.title: Re: How to disable "Post" authorization in app "Change Pa...
entry.content:
Read first the KBA 2401267 - Authorization check for enjoy parking transactions (FV50/FV60... 

  • Basically F_BKPF_BUK ACTVT activity 77 to park and 01 to post. read the KBA for more details and options.

entry.title: Überblick und Updates 2025 für SAP Training und Zertifizierungen
entry.content:
SAP Trainingsangebote und Lern-Formate 

Die SAP-Welt entwickelt sich ständig weiter, und mit ihr auch die Anforderungen an SAP Fachkräfte. Um diesem Wandel gerecht zu werden, bietet SAP ein breites Spektrum an Trainingsmöglichkeiten an. Von formellen Schulungen bis hin zu digitalen Lernformaten ist für jeden Lerntyp etwas dabei.  

Trainergeführte Kurse finden im Schulungszentrum, virtuell oder hybrid statt. Daneben gibt es Akademien und Schulungskonferenzen, die sich auf spezifische Themen wie Security oder Finanzen konzentrieren (diese werden im 2ten Halbjahr 2025 stattfinden). SAP bietet zudem maßgeschneiderte Schulungen vor Ort oder im Schulungszentrum an.  

Eine besonders flexible Option ist das "3 to Run"-Programm, bei dem Kunden einen Wunschtermin für eine Schulung vorschlagen können, sobald sich mindestens drei Teilnehmer gefunden haben. Dies ermöglicht eine bedarfsgerechte Terminplanung. Zur Übersicht über laufende Kurse bietet sich der SAP Trainings-Kalender an.  

Digitale SAP Lernangebote 

Im Bereich der digitalen Lernangebote hat SAP in den letzten Jahren stark investiert. Die Plattform learning.sap.com bietet eine Vielzahl kostenfreier Lerninhalte zum Selbststudium an. Hier wird zunehmend alles an Lern-Angeboten zentralisiert - so sind z.B. die openSAP Kurse schon in dem Bereich aufgegangen.  

Für ein noch umfassenderes Lernerlebnis steht der SAP Learning Hub zur Verfügung, das digitale Premium Lern-Angebot. Besonders hervorzuheben sind die über 600 Live-Sessions pro Quartal, die in verschiedenen Formaten angeboten werden, die Learning Analytics Dashboards sowie die mehr als 340 vorkonfigurierten Übungssysteme, die praktisches Hands-on-Learning ermöglichen. Auch sind vier Zertifizierungsversuche inkludiert, sowie das Ablegen einer Rezertifizierung. 

Neue SAP Schulungen und Lernangebote 

Für 2025 sind zahlreiche neue Angebote geplant. Neue Kurse gibt es beispielsweise zu Themen wie Terraform auf SAP BTP, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Administration of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (ADM001) oder Introduction to SAP Business Technology Platform Administration (ADMCLD).  

Ein Highlight ist auch der Change Management Workshop SAP Change Basecamp, in dem Change Management Aktivitäten entlang einer  Fallstudie erarbeitet werden.  

Auch gibt es Updates in den Übungssystemen.  So wurde das User Interface vereinfacht und der Zugang und die Navigation zu den Kursmaterialien auf der SAP Learning-Seite wurde angepasst, um Ihnen im Bereich „Mein Lernen“ zusätzliche Informationen zu bieten. Daneben gibt es regelmäßig neue Übungssysteme, so gibt es jetzt SAP Joule als Teil der SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sandbox. 

Informationen zu verschiedenen neuen Lernangeboten gibt es monatlich im SAP Learning Newsletter.    

SAP-Zertifizierung 

Im Bereich der SAP-Zertifizierung gab es in letzter Zeit größere Neuerungen. Der Fokus liegt nun stärker auf Rollen und Skills, statt nur auf Produktkenntnissen. Das Zertifizierungsangebot wurde gestrafft, um relevanter und übersichtlicher zu sein.  

Eine wichtige Änderung ist die Einführung des Stay-Certified Modells, bei dem eine jährliche Rezertifizierung erforderlich ist. Dies stellt sicher, dass Zertifizierte stets auf dem neuesten Stand der Technologie bleiben. Der Umfang ist jedoch mit ca. 20-30 Min. Prüfungszeit und unlimitierten Versuchen um einiges geringer wie bei einer initialen Zertifizierung, die schon 3 Stunden lange dauern kann.  

Für SAP-Partner gibt es neuerdings besondere Anforderungen. Bestimmte Schlüsselrollen in RISE-Projekten müssen bis Juni 2025 gültige Zertifizierungen vorweisen. Zudem müssen bis Ende 2025 50% der relevanten Partner-Ressourcen eine gültige SAP Solution Portfolio Zertifizierung haben. 

Eine innovative Neuerung ist das Format "Hands-on-Learning Real-World Validation". Hierbei müssen Teilnehmer für bestimmte Zertifizierungen praktische Übungen durchführen, um ihr Wissen in realen Szenarien unter Beweis zu stellen.  

Zur Vorbereitung für eine Zertifizierung ist auch die neue Get Certified Live Session-Serie hilfreich, in welchen - ergänzend zum Selbststudium über die digitalen Learning Journeys - erfahrene SAP Trainer die relevantesten Themen zu einer ausgewählten SAP-Zertifizierung in einer Reihe von Live-Sessions vorstellen und Tips und Tricks zur Prüfung teilen. Diese Get Certified Live Session Tracks führen die Lerner effizient durch die digitalen Learning Journeys. Die Neuerungen und vor allem viele Fragen & Antworten wurden hier im Blog zusammengefasst.  

Vorteilspakete für die SAP Weiterbildung 

SAP bietet verschiedene Vorteilspakete an. Die Preferred Card funktioniert wie eine Guthabenkarte mit einem Rabattmodell. Je höher die Vorauszahlung, desto größer der Rabatt. Das Learning Plus Package ist ein Rahmenvertrag für Schulungen und Zertifizierungen, der länderübergreifend genutzt werden kann und sich besonders für Großunternehmen eignet. Für beides gibt es derzeit übrigens noch weitere Prozente 

Ein besonders interessantes Angebot ist die SAP Training Flatrate. Für einen Jahresbetrag erhalten Teilnehmer unbegrenzten Zugang zu Klassenraumtrainings. Dies kann besonders für Juniorberater oder Neueinsteiger attraktiv sein, die sich intensiv in SAP-Themen einarbeiten möchten. Weitere Infos gibt es auf der Seite zu "Special Offers". 

Good-Practices rund um SAP Training  

Für ein effektives Lernen empfehlen wir folgende Best Practices: 

  • Nutzt die Learning Journeys zur Orientierung und zur Planung des individuellen Lernpfads. 
  • Kombiniert selbstgesteuertes E-Learning mit Live-Sessions oder Klassenraumtrainings für ein optimales Lernergebnis. 
  • Tauscht Euch in Communities aus und nutzt Sie die Möglichkeit, in Live-Sessions Fragen zu stellen. 
  • Übt praktisch in den Hands-on-Systemen, um theoretisches Wissen zu festigen und anzuwenden. 
  • Testet Euer Wissen über Zertifizierungen und regelmäßige Rezertifizierung. 

Zusammenfassung 

SAP hat sein Trainings- und Zertifizierungsangebot in den letzten Jahren erweitert und flexibilisiert. Von klassischen Schulungen bis hin zu digitalen Lernformaten gibt es je nach Lernbedürfnis verschiedene Möglichkeiten. Die Einführung des Stay Certified Modells und die neuen Anforderungen für Partner zeigen, dass aktuelle Expertise und lebenslanges Lernen immer wichtiger wird.  

Weitere Lern-Quellen und Events 

SAP bietet verschiedene Möglichkeiten, um auf dem Laufenden zu bleiben. Dazu gehört der monatliche Newsletter, die SAP Community Gruppe in deutsch und englisch und auf LinkedIn die Gruppe für SAP Training und Change Management. Ein besonderes Highlight ist das jährliche SAP Learning and Adoption Forum, das am 25. Juni in Walldorf und virtuell stattfindet.  

Weitere Links 

  • SAP Training und Zertifizierung auf sap.de 
  • SAP Learning Hub Informationen 
  • SAP Zertifizierung auf sap.de 
  • SAP Community Gruppe für Training und Change Management  
  • Englischsprachige SAP Learning Community 
  • Newsletter für SAP Schulungen 
  • Kostenfreies SAP Lernangebot: https://www.learning.sap.com 
  • SAP Kurskatalog 
  • Blog zu Live-Sessions im SAP Learning Hub  
  • SAP News zur Verpflichtung von Partnern, sich zu zertifizieren 
  • LinkedIn Gruppe zu SAP Training und Change 
  • Online Lernangebot zu SAP Build mit Hands-on Zertifizierung 
  • SAP Training & Change Community Treffen: monatliche Kaffee-Ecke 
  • SAP Change Management Basecamp 
  • SAP Training Forum 2025 am 25.06.

entry.title: Default to the legacy workbench, instead of the new applicatn workbench?
entry.content: Will the option to hide the toggle or set the default to the legacy workbench, instead of the new applicant workbench, be available during the 1H 2025 release?

entry.title: Best Practice for handling SUM log files
entry.content:
Background:

During the upgrade SUM generates log files. From the SUM guide, help docs and experiences I've learned, that...

  • SUM itself copies log files of past runs into a separate folder structure.
  • There's also some advice from SAP to cleanse old log files.

Now I wonder what is the actual best practice? How are you doing it?

I would assume...

  • the recently created or the older log files are taken off the fileshare and kept in a cheap storage location. Directly readable or in some compressed archive.
  • the files are kept for longer as they document what happened. Because some issues / warnings or situations might have gone unnoticed but raise issues later.
  • the files can help also in SAP support cases
  • the files are never deleted for above reasons or only deleted after x years or n further upgrades,...

Of some I've heard they didn't care about them, some throw them away real quick, some zip them directly after to a separate fileshare... it's wild.

Question:
Once a technical upgrade is completed - do you keep the log files created by SUM? How long? How far of reach? 
Please feel encouraged to share your experiences even if it's already listed by someone else.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Manage Substitution/Validation Rules – Journal Entries: A Guide to Business Contexts and Rules

Hello @MarcelvanD

Thanks,

Ciara

entry.title: Re: Downloads link in course accelerate-enterprise-integr...

entry.title: Issue with connection to postgresql from SAP CAP app in BAS
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

when i run the command cds deploy, i get the following error:

$ cds deploy --tunnel-address localhost:5432
/> deployment to localhost:5432 failed

TimeoutError: ResourceRequest timed out

 

I do have the ssh tunnel running on port 5432, the PostgreSQL instance is working fine when i connect to it from my local machine via DBeaver but for some reason i can not deploy changes, when inspecting the env via "cds env" i do see the correct username, password and database name, so i really dont understand why i can not cds deploy my tables.

entry.title: Re: About the Coffee Corner – JOIN now, it’s interesting and fun!
entry.content: Hello! It is great to meet you. Maybe we'll meet at SAP TechEd Berlin which will be here before we know it! Wishing you the best!

entry.title: Re: A letter to Otto – PM of the EarlyWatch Alert. I love you Otto
entry.content:
Hi Mario,

Thanks for your very kind letter to Otto, which my colleague Susanne spotted. 

SAP EarlyWatch Alert does exist for about 25 years and has developed and changed a lot.
It's a constant in your life and also during my work at SAP.

By the way, I assume that your weekly emails are sent from a SAP Solution Manager systems. I would recommend to consume SAP EarlyWatch Alert directly in SAP for Me, which provides more insights and you benefit from all recent innovations. Access all SAP EarlyWatch Alert related applications in SAP for Me https://me.sap.com/servicessupport/reporting

  • https://me.sap.com/ewa/workspace
  • https://me.sap.com/ewa/report
  • https://me.sap.com/ewasop/soopDetail (Dashboard)
  • https://me.sap.com/ewa/solutionfinder
  • https://me.sap.com/ewa/status

I will forward your blog to the product management of the SAP Readiness Check. 
By the way, the SAP Readiness Check has a nice UI, too.
SAP Readiness Check | SAP Help Portal

Ruediger Stoecker 
(Area Product Owner of SAP EarlyWatch Alert Workspace in SAP for Me)

entry.title: Sorry, the draft cannot be activated right now. Please try again later.
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I'm trying to add custom field in FIORI apps Manage Revenue Contract, after I add my custom field from Adapt UI function and click activate, got pop up error saying "Sorry, the draft cannot be activated right now. Please try again later." 

I try to follow this solution but I cannot found any asterisk(*).

Appreciate your help on this. Thanks

entry.title: Re: Is there a way we can measure how many times we ran o...
entry.content:
Thanks for your suggestions @Vaibhav-Mishra ,

Options 1 and 2 aren't viable in my opinion because it relies on people to record events. Which in my experience is not a reliable source for indicators unless it is the only way possible.  If it is the only possible option, then strong process, technical solution and training are required to make it work.

I'd rather continue looking for possible ways of capturing this metric through existing processes, technologies and user knowledge to define a metric that will work for us.

Our development in ECC is minimal on Plant Maintenance module because we are planning S4 migration and want to avoid any complications related to Z* objects.

Hence my question to @Oscar_DC about S4 capabilities in this area.

entry.title: Re: Redis on SAP BTP - PostgreSQL Triggers Updating Redis with PL/pgSQL
entry.content:
Hi @antoniojmnunes ,

Just curiosity, is there any reason why SAP BTP Provides this outdated service?, I mean that is not longer maintained, because Redis moved from Open Source( 3-Clause BSD ) to Source-Available (RSALv2/SSPLv1) one year ago.

Is not a better idea just to provide forks like ValKey ? 😉

Kind Regards.

Max.

entry.title: Payroll end of year new hire
entry.content:
Hi,

We run monthly payroll.  1st to 31st March is paid on 25th March.  

After we had finalised March payroll, FPS submitted to HMRC, payroll is in exit etc; we are then advised by our customer that we had a new hire on 3rd March which we had not earlier been advised of.  This employee therefore does not have March payroll results.

I understand that an FPSA is not possible since there are not payroll results for March to amend.

 

We are being told that we should include this payment along with the April payment ensuring that it is reported as 2 payments in one pay period.

I cannot believe that the system is not somehow capable to send YTD values which should have been included in 24/25?  Is this correct?

The March salary will therefore be included in the 25/26 YTDs?

entry.title: SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library Unavailable due to Outdated SAPUI5 Version?
entry.content:
Hi,

It seems that the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library is currently unavailable due to an outdated version of SAPUI5. When trying to access the app via https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/, it fails to load and appears to be stuck in a loading state.

A quick look at the network traffic revealed that the app attempts to load the file https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/1.96.27/resources/sap-ui-core.js, but this version of SAPUI5 is outdated and no longer exists under this url, resulting in a 404 NOT FOUND error.

Could someone at SAP kindly update the SAPUI5 version in the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library so we can use it again?

Thank you in advance for your assistance!

entry.title: Re: Mass Update of Contact Person - Customer Master Data...
entry.content:
Hi,

have you tried VAP1 transaction to create a new contact person?

Best regards

entry.title: SAP Build Apps: REST API Detail Page: Unexpected '?0=' Query Parameter Issue
entry.content:
Builders,

I'm encountering an issue while setting up a detail page using a REST API. I've configured a Data Variable that relies on a Page Parameter passed from the previous page to populate a Query Parameter for the API.

The API functions correctly in Postman, supporting all CRUD operations. Specifically, retrieving a single entry via GET requires a URL like:

https://*********.accounts.ondemand.com/scim/Users/62f75cbf-0b3a-40b6-9b326-e8c203426

However, when Build Apps constructs the URL, it appends ?0=, resulting in:

 

This additional ?0= seems to be causing the API to return all records instead of the specific record identified by the Page Parameter. I've confirmed this behavior by manually constructing the same URL in Postman.

My Questions:

  1. Is there a workaround to prevent Build Apps from appending ?0= to the URL?
  2. Alternatively, are there other methods to filter the results on the detail page to display the correct record, given that this is a REST API (not OData)?

I've observed that most detail page examples utilize OData, which might have different handling. Any insights or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Renewal of CN=*.cockpit.btp.cloud.sap
entry.content:
Hello! I would like to clarify a certificate change related to SAP BTP (CN=*.cockpit.btp.cloud.sap vs CN=emea.cockpit.btp.cloud.sap) and check if it affects trust settings in our subaccount. Is it possible to get guidance without opening a support incident?

We are currently using the certificate with the following subject:

CN=*.cockpit.btp.cloud.sap, O=SAP SE, L=Walldorf, SP=Baden-Württemberg.

We have identified a new certificate with the subject:

CN=emea.cockpit.btp.cloud.sap

Could you please confirm the following:

  1. Can the new certificate (CN=emea.cockpit.btp.cloud.sap) be used as a replacement for the existing wildcard certificate (*.cockpit.btp.cloud.sap)?
  2. Will this certificate change affect current SSL communication or truststore configurations in SAP BTP or SAP Integration Suite?
  3. Is there any action required on our side to ensure the trust relationship remains valid (e.g. importing new root/intermediate certificates)?
  4. Is this change part of a planned update from SAP (e.g. change in Certificate Authority or regional separation)?
  5. Currently this certificate does not exist in STUST QAS - only in DEV and PRD. I wonder if it is also necessary to include it in QAS in the SSL Client (Anonymous)?

Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: Re: Account determination error in MIGO
entry.content:
Read this document from support content: Error M8147 - Account Determination for entry not possible 

  • Look also for blog/help/guide such as What is Account Assignment Category in SAP MM, How to configure?

entry.title: Re: How to create RKF (Restricted key Figure) In SAC Add-...
entry.content:
@Vasu_Sac 

Currently There is no way to create any calculated measure (Restricted/calculated) in Excel Add-in. You will have to define these calculation at model level and then consume it in Excel.

 

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: SAP DRC, Peppol / Error at PUSH operation HTTP response code: 500
entry.content: we have the same problems. Do you get a solution?

entry.title: Query to show "Production Order" and related "Issue for Production" with bin locations
entry.content:
Hi, I'm trying to write a query in SAP B1 HANA to show a production order along with its Issue for Production including the Bin Location but whenever I try the bin location is blank?
Any help appreciated.
This is where I got to:
SELECT
T1."ItemCode", T1."ItemName", T3."BinCode", T0."TransNum", T0."ItemCode" AS "IssueItem", T2."OnHandQty" AS "QuantityInBin"
FROM "OINM" T0
INNER JOIN "OITM" T1 ON T0."ItemCode" = T1."ItemCode"
INNER JOIN "OIBQ" T2 ON T0."ItemCode" = T2."ItemCode"
INNER JOIN "OBIN" T3 ON T2."BinAbs" = T3."AbsEntry"
WHERE T0."TransType" = '59'
But I need to start with the Production order and link in the Issue for Production.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Leverage Input parameters from CDS in Datasphere
entry.content:
Parameterized CDS views don't support to SAP Datasphere but those are support to SAC.

BW4HANA side also don't support Parameterized CDS views.

Those are created for embedded analytics purpose.

entry.title: Reporting on all Indirect Reports with Story Reporting
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

 My client wishes to have a report to count all the direct / indirect reports, all the levels down. Is there an option similar to OrgChart where it can show the direct and indirect reports of a manager(Ex: 12/150) in story reporting?

Example:

Stephan is a Board member and is the top of the hierarchy (employe’s manager = NO_MANAGER). He has 1 direct report (Laura).

Laura is a CEO and has 5 direct reports.

Each of Laura's direct reports has 5 direct reports of their own.

The report I need would counts the following:

Laura has 30 reports (5 direct and 25 indirect). 

Stephan has 31 reports (1 direct and 30 indirect).

Thank you,

Miruna

entry.title: Re: Is there a way we can measure how many times we ran o...
entry.content:
hello @pascal_bellerose2 ,

You can create a CDS services which is possible in ECC. If you are using HANA database in ECC then go with CDS views. The CDS will be updated as per the transaction events of non availability of spare parts and can be called in a report. Later on when you migrate to S4HANA then it is easy to migrate with CDS services/ views. In S4HANA same can be called in a Fiori report. You can refer this QnA post.  

Vaibhav

entry.title: Issue with External Data Resource Not Returning Data in CDS View after Adding to Project
entry.content:
Hi, I am facing an issue with fetching external data from the SAP system’s catalog of services.

Here’s the scenario:

  1. I added the service by creating a new service of type ABAP catalog and connected it using on-prem connection through Cloud Connector. Once created, a destination was generated 

  2.  

  3. Then, I went to the Storyboard and added an external resource from the SAP system. I selected my service ZC_PURCHASEORDER_FS_SRV. In this step, I can see the live data,

  4. After that, I pressed "Add to Project".

  5. I created a service and imported a projection using the CDS file created in the external service.

  6. When I run the project using CDS W, I get no data returned.

  7.  

So, I’m wondering if I missed a step or if there’s something else that could be causing the issue as it worked with me in sap build apps. Has anyone encountered a similar issue, or could you point out what I might be doing wrong?

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: How to create a OData Service in Public Cloud?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I've questions below.

How to create an OData Service in SAP Public Cloud?

Where do I find the GET Entity method and GET EntitySet Method?

Where do I pass the Input JSON for the POST method?

I want to create an OData with CRUD operations in the SAP Public Cloud.

Can anyone suggest the best practice?

Thanks,

Naveen.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition  

OData

entry.title: Re: LMS HRBP/Manager Team List
entry.content: We were zoomed out. It just wasn't dialing further into the hierarchy.

entry.title: Re: SAPScript Priting in Heavy (bit think) A4 paper - Printer Error
entry.content:
Hi!

 

I have the same problem on a Ricoh-printer. The windows-driver has a setting for the thickness of the paper. Printing in Windows is working.

 

When I print in SAP on the same Windows-Printer, it doesn't work, error message is that the paper doesn't fit because of thickness. I think SAP is overruling this.

Any ideas how to solve this?

 

br

Peter Piry

entry.title: Re: How to change field position in output display of tra...
entry.content:
In S_ALR_87012357 transaction initial screen, open the 'Output List' there you can configure every report.

(or use BAdI FI_TAX_BADI_014 to update field catalog)

entry.title: Three Reasons SAP BusinessObjects Still Matters in 2025
entry.content:
In a previous article, I answered the question "Is BusinessObjects Still Relevant in 2025?" with a resounding yes, giving a number of reasons why the platform still matters. In this article, I provide three reasons why the SAP BusinessObjects platform still matters in 2025.

Rich Semantic Layer

The first reason SAP BusinessObjects still matters in 2025 is its rich semantic layers. Whether we are talking about a classic BusinessObjects universe or the semantic layers provided by BW (SAP Business Warehouse),SAP HANA, or SAP Datasphere, they enable self-service analytics. These semantic layers need to be redesigned or fudged when building analytics with a non-SAP toolset. Competing semantic layers often lack the flexibility and power already provided by SAP BusinessObjects. The time and effort involved in reinventing the semantic layer should be carefully estimated before reaching for another analytics tool. And in the case of the universe, it provides a layer of isolation between the data model and the user. This isolation is why organizations can move their enterprise data warehouse from an on-premise platform to a cloud platform, carefully update the existing universe with zero disruption to the user community. 

Scheduling and Publishing

The second reason SAP BusinessObjects still matters in 2025 is its scheduling and publishing capabilities. Information distribution is often an immature feature in "modern" analytics tools, whose vendors are quietly introducing "new" features like- wait for it- tables for users who prefer a "classic" BI experience. Of course, third parties are bringing extensions to fill these gaps. But getting the data into a table is only part of the solution - being able to burst the information in an efficient and personalized way is equally important.

Data Mode

The third reason SAP BusinessObjects still matters in 2025 is its new Data Mode. Introduced in BI 4.3 and enhanced in BI 2025, Data Mode brings self-service data blending and data cleansing to SAP BusinessObjects - Web Intelligence (WebI) users without introducing a separate product or vendor for that purpose. And Data Mode isn't simply a feature. It's a sign of SAP's renewed commitment to the SAP BusinessObjects platform. As witnessed in last month's release of SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence 2025, SAP has done far more than just slap a new About Box on an aging product. From Fiori to UI5 charting to Data Mode to cloud database support, the SAP BusinessObjects platform has been thoroughly modernized where it really counts

Conclusion

It's not wrong to desire to pick the best tool for the job, or to have multiple tools to choose from. But analytics leaders need to look honestly at some of the poorly designed, poorly performing deliverables that have been created on their watch with the "latest and greatest" tools. And reconsider the total cost of ownership and return on investment in choosing a familiar yet thoroughly modern solution that they are often tempted to overlook.

entry.title: Community project: Batch input toolbox for ABAP
entry.content:
Unfortunately batch input processing still matters... Here is a tool to help keep batch input programming cleaner and more readable.

How it works

The idea is to capture the transaction run we want to automate in an object.

ta = new zcl_tool_bi_transaction( 'SO10' ).

Pass the transaction to call as a parameter to the constructor.

Then create inputs screen by screen

ta->new_dynpro(
  )->program( 'SAPMSSCE'
  )->dynpro( '1100'
  )->cursor( 'RSSCE-TDNAME'
  )->ok_code( '=CREA'
  )->field( 'RSSCE-TDNAME' )->value( |{ textname }|
  )->field( 'RSSCE-TDID' )->value( 'ST'
  )->field( 'RSSCE-TDSPRAS' )->value( 'EN' ).
ta->new_dynpro(
  )->program( 'SAPLSTXX'
  )->dynpro( '1100'
  )->ok_code( '=TXSV'
  )->line( 2 )->field( 'RSTXT-TXLINE' )->value( 'First line'
  )->line( 2 )->cursor( 'RSTXT-TXLINE'
  )->line( 3 )->field( 'RSTXT-TXPARGRAPH' )->value( '*'
  )->line( 3 )->field( 'RSTXT-TXLINE' )->value( 'Second line'
  )->line( 4 )->field( 'RSTXT-TXPARGRAPH' )->value( '*'
  )->line( 4 )->field( 'RSTXT-TXLINE' )->value( 'third line' ).

Set the dynpro with `program( )` and `dynpro( )`.

field( <name> )->value( <value> ) will set the value in a field. You can pass character-like values as well as date fields or numerical fields. The tool will try to convert the data to BDC_FVAL accordingly.

line( <index> )->field( <name> )->value( <value> ) sets the value in a loop field, pass the line index as parameter.

Set the user command with ok_code( ).

After having set up all screen data, go on and run the transaction:

    if session is not initial.
      call function 'BDC_OPEN_GROUP'
        exporting
          group = session
        importing
          qid   = session_id.
      if ta->insert( logger ) > 0.
        logger->popup( ).
      else.
        call function 'BDC_CLOSE_GROUP'.
      endif.
    else.
      if ta->mode( mode )->run( logger ) > 0.
        logger->popup( ).
      endif.
    endif.

As you see, there is an option to use a batch input session as well as run the transaction directly.

Before running, you can set parameters with

  • mode( ) - BI mode A/N/E
  • no_bi_mode( ), no_bi_end( )
  • set_defsize( ) - use default size window
  • update( ) - set update mode

Dependencies

This package uses ABAP Logger 

You can install the package with ABAPGIT from github

entry.title: Re: Is there a way we can measure how many times we ran o...
entry.content: Thanks for that info, I'll keep that in mind if we ever come up with a proper set of rules that will detect events and populate such view.

entry.title: Re: SAP DRC, Peppol / Error at PUSH operation HTTP response code: 500
entry.content:
Hi S_WI,

There were two types of issues:

  1. The web service had been activated for the old PEPPOL version, so we had to deactivate and reactivate it.

  2. One PEPPOL node was using an outdated certificate, which the node operator needed to update.

Hope this helps!

Best regards,
Vasili
NexQuent

entry.title: Re: How to install python libraries PIL, pytesseract, openCV, pdf2image in SAP Cloud Foundry?
entry.content: is it possible to install dependencies like the tesseract.exe executable and poppler (which is required for pdf2image) via requirements.txt ?

entry.title: SAP SRM connection to SAP Public cloud s/4 hana system
entry.content:
Hello Community, 

I  have received a query from one of our financial backend systems which are doing migration from the current ECC system to the SAP Public Cloud-based S/4 system.

The current ECC system is connected to the SAP SRM for procurement functions via RFC 

After their migration to the public cloud, we need to assess the technical feasibility of running all current business scenarios from SAP SRM.

Are there any standard process / guidelines to connect SAP SRM to public or private cloud-based S/4 systems?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Allocation Number on Bank Statement SAP HANA
entry.content:
Hello everyone

I'm in the Bank Statement Management application F1564, where I ran the transaction and entered the assignment number as 0. However, when I check FB03, it saves a different assignment number. I made the correction manually.

Bank Statement

FB03

Is that number generated automatically? How can I correct it so it shows the bank statement assignment number?

SAP S/4HANA Finance

entry.title: Delivery doesn't pick from Q-Stock as intended for NB2 Return Purchase Orders (S/4HANA OnPrem)
entry.content:
We are facing an issue in the following process:

We receive goods from a supplier, and the stock is initially posted to quality inspection stock (Q-stock). Since the goods are defective, we want to return them to the supplier. For this, we create an NB2 return purchase order.
In the PO item, the stock type is manually changed to stock type X. By default, it is set to “Unrestricted-Use Stock”. Oddly, this manual change is not saved immediately. Only after reopening and editing the NB2 PO again and re-selecting stock type X does the setting remain in the document.

After that, we create outbound deliveries for the NB2 PO in order to return the goods to the supplier. However, in the outbound delivery, the system looks at unrestricted-use stock (where the quantity is 0), instead of checking the Q-stock (where the quantity is 20) – even though the delivery type is actually configured to look at Q-stock by default.

Has anyone experienced a similar issue?
Is there an explanation for why the delivery is looking at unrestricted-use stock?
Is there a way to ensure the outbound delivery picks from Q-stock as expected?
The only related SAP Note I found is 3528419, but as far as I can tell, the stock type can indeed be changed in the NB2 PO.

Thanks in advance for any ideas or insights!

entry.title: Re: SAP DRC, Peppol / Error at PUSH operation HTTP respon...
entry.content: Thank you!

entry.title: Creating a CDS views
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to create a CDS view which will show by the end number of purchase order done by each year. I have developed the following codes but the result doesn't give all the years collected together.

Can someone tell me whats wrong with this code:

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZPORDCBYYR'

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED

@EndUserText.label: 'Purchase Order Count By Year'

define view Z_POrdCByYr

as select from ekko

{

key substring (aedat,1,4) as PurOrdrYear,

count (distinct ebeln) as OrderCount

}

group by

aedat

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Timetracking integration with SAP S/4H...

entry.title: Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, and SAP generative AI Hub
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, SAP AI Core and SAP generative AI Hub. It is beneficial if you have knowledge about JavaScript as we're using the SAP Cloud SDK for AI with the JavaScript variant. You should also have good knowledge about the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model.

This CodeJam introduces attendees to aspects of exposing generative artificial intelligence (genAI) capabilities to execute tasks using a given business context.

During this event, you'll become acquainted with the theory behind genAI, SAP AI Core, SAP AI Launchpad, and learn how to use the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model to expose AI capabilities through your own OData service.

While working through the exercises, you'll become comfortable creating CDS-based service definitions and integrating them into the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and SAP AI Core Foundation Models.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Friday April 25th, 2025 at the SAP offices in Newtown Square, USA from 09:30 am - 3:30 pm. The workshop will be hosted by SAP America Inc. The event will be held in english.

The instructor will be @johna69 and Nana Korsah.

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Google Chrome.

Please note that seating is limited - we can accommodate 25 attendees. Register here in the Community Event using the RSVP functionality.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Revenue Accounting and Reporting - Event based fulfillment
entry.content:
Hello there,

I'm having trouble gaining details on functionality related to Revenue Accounting and Reporting, specifically the mechanism that triggers revenue recognition. I have a requirement that cannot look to percentage of revenue/cost incurred to drive revenue recognition, rather the progress of a WBS element/milestone being confirmed at complete. By virtue of this, I've ruled out EBRR as each method seemed to rely on the percentage of either cost/revenue.

I've then started to research RAR and the "event-based fulfillment" of performance obligations. (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/f63dd39a28bb4b90adbf9e608aff58ea/c5ce0b1d7ca34fabb4b...)

In referencing the help document, I've found the information to be vague and not detailed enough to understand if any event-based fulfillment methods will look to WBS elements as a source of information to calculate revenue recognition. 

I have two questions, is there someone in our community that has extensive knowledge to RAR, and its methods of calculating revenue in a project based process order. Does SAP have any documents that provide more detail on how these methods work and the accounting entries they drive?

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Integration scenarios with SAP S/4HANA Transportation Management
entry.content:
Hello Michael
i wonder if you are aware of this integration that is on the roadmap of S/4HANA 2021:
"Ability to integrate SAP S/4HANA Supply Chain for transportation management with MRP live".

This is the only place i have found something about this integration, but this feature is not really documented (at least i can not find under these topic names)
https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=089E017A62AB1EDAA6CCB767DDDCC0E5&q=MRP&range=FIRST-LAST#;INNO=000D3AAC9DD21EECA2E40687A81AE1F4
I was looking for  inbound load optimization features with TM (optimizing the PR Quantities ) according to load and inventory range.
Best regards 
Philipp

entry.title: Re: A Multivariate Time Series Modeling and Forecasting Guide with Python Machine Learning Client fo

and am quite irritated since the sequence of test is not possible.
Granger demads stationary data.
Therefore Adfuller and differenciation has to take place befor that with the result that the Granger has complety different results.

Has there been any changes in the meantime?
Also the code snipets do not really seem to be connected.
Adfuller want no date but float, suddenly pandas and numpy are used .

Do I get something wrong?

Thanks for response.
Dirk

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library Unavailable due to Outdated SAPUI5 Version?
entry.content: If it happens with SAP... it happens with customers...

entry.title: Re: EWM opportunistic cross docking and POSC
entry.content:
Hi @MarekKaminski ,

I guess its because of quality steps , not sure, Please check below link and let us know if any questions.

"

Note

  • For the cross-docking, EWM only considers inbound delivery items for which goods receipt has been confirmed.

    EWM does not consider inbound delivery items that are relevant for the quality inspection."

  • Source Documentation : https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/9832125c23154a179bfa1784cdc9577a/92d39a281861405696552d7b5340f81a.html

For your case, cross-dock should be trigger before putaway once QC... steps are completed, right ?

Prime facie you need enhancement/ BADI. Obsevation-Cross dock should trigger once quality clearWE-ZONE-IC instead of WE-ZONE before putaway, however Cross docking system triggered at WE-ZONE in standard.

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Re: Release Highlights of S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition for Public Sector (US), 2302
entry.content:
Hi Jaap, Recordsmanagement was a "cross" basis functionality and not restricted to PS customers.

Public Edition follows  the ECM strategy for now and the same goes for Public Sector customers..
With Enterprise Contract Management, you can integrate an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system with other contract assembly products (for example, SAP Enterprise Contract Assembly), to enable users to create and manage legal content that is generated out of various business scenarios in a company, such as procurement processes, sales, policies or intercompany agreements.
You can also manage contexts. See FSD  chapter 4.3.2 https://help.sap.com/doc/7c9e0bbbd1664c2581b2038a1c7ae4b3/2502.500/en-US/FSD_CE2502.pdf

or application docu: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/ff01156df84f470cb46483b8a0ba0b79/c85c7b4e7b3849b9b58ac8282de8cef4.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: How to call Function Import of external OData for fet...
entry.content:
Instead of passing parameter as object

    const result = await BDService.send("GetPDF",parameters,headers);

pass as string literal

 

  let result = await BDService.send('GET', `/GetPDF?BillingDocument='${docNo}'`);

entry.title: ETL Objects in SAP Datasphere and Dataflows Example
entry.content:
Hello Friends,

Welcome back to my other serios of blog post on an exciting feature available in SAP Datasphere, i.e., the data flows in SAP Datasphere. In this blog post, we will understand this concept with an example. 

First, let us understand the different ETL objects available in SAP Datasphere, and in this blog, we will

mainly discuss dataflows using Python scripts.

Datasphere ETL Objects:

Data Flows 

Data flow is a key component of SAP Datasphere as it allows users to perform complex data transformations, enrich data, and structure it for reporting and analytics.

In this blog, we will explore the basic features and steps to create a data flow in SAP Datasphere.

A Data Flow is a graphical ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) tool that allows users to:

✔ Extract data from various sources (SAP and non-SAP).
✔ Apply transformations such as filtering, aggregations, joins, and calculated columns.
✔ Load transformed data into target tables for reporting and analytics.

 

Remote Tables:

Replication flows:

Transformation flows:

Task chains:

Let us discuss an example about dataflows using a Python script operator. Rest operator examples are written by others in their blog posts.

Now get set and go.

Please understand the different operators provided in SAP Datasphere. Currently, SAP Datasphere does not have a full-fledged ETL capacity, just like SAP BODS does. But in future releases, SAP data intelligence will be added as a feature that can be used in E2E ETL scenarios.

Python Script 

Supported Python Libraries as of now in SAP Datasphere:

Nympy and Panda. 

https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/user_guide/index.html#user-guide

https://numpy.org/learn/  

The Python script operator area is not as mature as the Jupyter notebook and is not so user-friendly. If you face any syntax error, it is a little tricky to find out the error unless and until we execute the dataflow to see the exact error in the data integration monitor. Hope SAP can integrate the Jupiter notebook IDE with SAP DataSphere with many more libraries to support so that the data science and AI capabilities can be further explored.

I will cover the error handling part and the running/scheduling of dataflow in a separate topic under data integration monitor topic.

Target table and options.

Now, deploy and run the dataflow. This is how we create a dataflow in SAP Datasphere. Also, we can schedule the dataflow, or we can integrate with a task chain and schedule the task chain as well with other flows.

Points to be noted and limitations with dataflows are :

When we create a new remote table, it is created virtually using the data federation mechanism. Original data stays in the remote source system, and SAP Datasphere just points to that table.

We cannot use the remote table directly inside a dataflow. We need to create a view on top of that remote table and then consume the view inside a dataflow.

Kindly test this scenario from your side as well. Thank you for reading this blog post, and I hope you liked the content.

watch out for next set of topics in coming days

Thanks,

Narasingha

entry.title: When will prices for SQL Anywhere on the SAP Online Store be fixed?
entry.content:
The price for all SQL Anywhere license options on the SAP Online store seems fixed at $495 USD.  For example, that is the price for "SAP SQL Anywhere, advanced edition (core)" and "SAP SQL Anywhere, remote database client". 

I discussed this with a SAP representative in the Store Chat.  The representative said she would notify the appropriate people and keep me up to date via email.  I have heard nothing.  

This problem seems new after migrating the E-Commerce provider to CleverBridge.  

I may very well need to send a customer to the SAP Online store.  Right now I can't!

entry.title: Re: It doesn't enable the copy button in discover
entry.content: Is Account Type: CUSTOMER

entry.title: Benefits of Bing𝐗 Refer Code in 2025 "ZMSZ1G"
entry.content: It serves as a portal to explore a platform renowned for its innovation and stringent security measures. To register for "ZMSZ1G"

entry.title: Year end revenue adjustment automatically in Public cloud
entry.content: We want to check with if the year-end deferrals/accrual adjustments in the preceding year can be configured to be automatically reversed the following year? For example, the deferrals/accruals generated in the system in December 2024 can be automatically reverse in January 2025. If cannot be automatically reverse, how can we reverse the YE deferral/accrual in P/L that is currently lying in the balance sheet account. please help.

entry.title: Re: Returns process for advanced intercompany sales (5D2)
entry.content:
Hi, we've set up 2F4 in combination with 5D2, like suggested.

Unfortunately, some documents are missing and this process is unsuitable for this.

We're now trying to implement returns as suggested (BKP) here using points 1-3, but I get a message immediately upon creating the customer returns that the supplier, so the business partner representing the plant, is not suitable for customer returns. What is SAP's approach to resolving this? I just read on SAP Help, that it is not allowed using internal business partners for that scenario. Maybe I have to activate any further Scope item?

entry.title: Re: Country dimension for Expense Reporting not working for Subledger and Concur
entry.content: Hi Virendra,
